thinking:
**Analyzing Visual Cues**

I'm currently dissecting the image and style instructions. My focus is on replicating the provided tab design precisely. Furthermore, I'm noting the specific request for the back button: it needs to be purple, matching the website's color scheme, with a white icon.


**Refining Tab Aesthetics**

I'm now zeroing in on the tab styling. The key is in the visual distinctions between active and inactive tabs. The active tab needs a vibrant purple background and rounded edges, with a subtle shadow. Inactive tabs are transparent with dark gray text, and no border. I will make sure the text structure will be "ETAPA (QTD)" and it is all upper-cased. My current code needs refinement to accurately render the requested styles.


**Adjusting Tab Structure**

I'm now revising the tab text formatting based on the example image. The selected tab uses all-caps text, like "TODOS." Unselected tabs are formatted as "ETAPA (QTD)" where the quantity count is in parentheses and directly follows the stage name. My focus is on adjusting the current code to reflect this precise formatting.


**Revising Tab Rendering**

I'm now focusing on how tabs render, specifically their active/inactive states. I need to make the text formatting precise. Inactive tabs will display "STAGE (COUNT)" format; active tabs are just the stage name in all caps. The current code will need to be adjusted to accommodate this visual distinction. I am also planning to change the count display to include it always.


**Implementing Tab State**

I'm now integrating the stage name with its count, as the image shows, using all caps for the stage name and parentheses around the count. Active tabs get a purple pill design with a shadow effect, and inactive tabs are transparent with gray text, which darkens on hover. I'm focusing on aligning the visual states with the active/inactive logic. Furthermore, I'm integrating draggable state with opacity change for a better user experience.


**Enhancing Tab Integration**

I'm now integrating design elements for a more cohesive look. Since active tabs are pill-shaped, I'll extend this style to other elements, specifically buttons on the right. I plan to change the external div to `rounded-full` and remove the inactive border, to give it that pill shape. The addition button will also get a pill-style and match the styling. The tab text is all-caps and bold in the image, so I'm updating to use the correct font weight and style.


**Modifying Visual Elements**

I'm now implementing the integration of visual elements based on active tab states. Specifically, I'm reviewing the current code snippet provided. For active tabs, I'm integrating the text structure `{colName} ({count})` and actions which are only available to the active tabs. Furthermore, I'll modify the back button: its background color will be changed to purple (`bg-[#533af6]`) and the icon's color will be white.


**Refining Back Button**

I'm now implementing the refined back button style. It needs a purple background (`bg-[#533af6]`), white icon (`text-white`), rounded appearance (`rounded-full`), and a shadow. I've removed conflicting borders and added hover effects. I'm verifying the code to ensure this is the intended back button, confirming it aligns with the user's latest request.


**Investigating Code Functionality**

I'm now focusing on a code search within `CompanyDashboard.tsx` to locate instances of `setSelectedJob(null)`. I'll avoid the system's `grep_search` due to potential compatibility issues. I am leaning toward a script or file viewing for safety and accuracy. My plan is to locate and verify the specific locations where this function is called.

args.Description: "Script para buscar ocorrências de setSelectedJob(null) e termos de voltar no CompanyDashboard"
args.toolSummary: "Cria um script node para localizar o botão de voltar no código"
